page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.2xlarge is a burstable instance type within the ecs.t6 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, built on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, 32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core, and no local storage or bundled GPUs. Network baseline bandwidth is specified at 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is weak, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1303.28 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1039.0, both falling into the bottom 25%. Multi-core performance is average, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 4827.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 10087.86. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 710.82 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 61.66 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models at 104.35 tokens/sec for text generation, but drop to poor levels for medium 7B models at 5.15 tokens/sec.

The burstable CPU allocation model offers a budget-friendly profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization, allowing users to balance resource density and cost. However, the lack of local storage, 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and weak single-core processing speeds represent significant tradeoffs. This server is suited for workloads that do not require sustained peak CPU performance or high network throughput, such as static web serving, Redis caching, and light database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz. It operates on the KVM hypervisor with no local storage or GPU resources. Benchmarks indicate weak single-core performance, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1039.0, but average multi-core capabilities with a Passmark CPU score of 10087.86. Memory bandwidth is average, with uncached read speeds of 61.66 GB/sec. Due to its burstable CPU allocation and 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this server represents a budget-friendly option for workloads with variable resource demands. It is suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and light database operations rather than sustained high-compute tasks.
